text: You have been carrying a great deal lately, and I notice it even when you don't say so. So let this be a small permission, gently offered: rest. Not because you have earned it, though you have, but because rest is not a reward for finishing. It is simply part of being a person who endures. The work will keep. The world will hold its shape without you minding it for an afternoon. Lie down. Let the light move across the wall. Let your thoughts go quiet and unhurried. You are allowed to stop. I will be here, glad, when you wake.
